feedback.text: Hello, I would like to write to inform you about the states of Videos Games survivance nowadays. Recently, the "Stop Killing Game" initiatives brought to the table that more and more Video Games are killed or to be killed. There's many reasons linked to it. Games being fully online, Games being replaced (Overwatch 1 for instant), Games not being supported anymore or even Games which need online support to be full. And that's only few reasons among others. These problems didn't appeared because nobody could do anything about it. There is solutions. For instant, for online Video Games, servers can be hosted by player themselves. Games which need online support to be full usually could be on Disc, not in the more optimal way but still be playable. And it's one little part of the problems existing and the many more solutions which could prevent them. For Old Games, the software could be compatible with them, many software did it. Of course, these problems didn't appeared by themselves. More and more enterprises decided not to prevent them, to let them happen because of the money it would cost. Leading to result when Video Games disappear and become unplayable legally. Plus, when confronted with these situations, they usually turn a deaf ear or warp the requests. For instant, the CEO of Ubisoft saying that no Games can be infinitely supported. Something that nobody asked but easily understable for anyone listening to him. With his mindset, we succesfully got "Concord" from Ubisoft, a mainly online Video Game which lived for few weeks before being turned off. Making it a overprice 80 Euros brick for anyone who bought it. Without forgetting that physic copy can still be bought while turned of. Nowadays, Videos Games are part of our culture, some are recognised piece of art. But with the mindset of these enterprise, we let our culture disappear because of people not wanting to put their effort in making sure it survives. That's what I'm asking to EU to make sure enterprise have to at least make game playable even after their end of support. If you wanna know more about this situation, you may check the "Stop Killing Games" initiative, thanks for reading me and have a nice day !
